The campground exists and is a national forest campground, not a state park as initially described. The official name is Hemlock Campground and Boat Launch.

notes: The campground is a real and verified State Park, officially named Keith J. Charters Traverse City State Park. The official Michigan DNR website confirms its existence and provides details.

The campground 'Crystal Shore Municipal Campground' appears to be fabricated. A verified, city-owned campground named 'Munising Tourist Park Campground' exists at this location.

The campground name 'Cedar Dune Lakeside Camp' appears to be fabricated. There is no record of a campground with this name in Muskegon, MI. The likely intended campground is Muskegon State Park, which is located in Muskegon and fits the description of a state park near dunes and a lake.
The provided campground name was incorrect. The actual campground is the Oscoda / Tawas KOA Holiday, which was verified through the official KOA website and other sources.

The campground 'New Swamp County Park' appears to be fabricated. However, a real campground named 'Ottawa Sands County Park' exists in the same area and is operated by Ottawa County. The camping area within the park is called 'Idema Explorers Camp'.
The campground is a real state park in Michigan, verified through the official Michigan DNR website. The park is temporarily closed for enhancements until at least May 15, 2026.

The campground name 'Lake Lakeside Camp' appears to be fabricated. A thorough search revealed the correct campground at this location is Rocky Fork State Park Campground, which is managed by the Ohio Department of Natural Resources.
